title: Antirhea affinis
taxonomy: Antirhea affinis belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is part of the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the apocynum, asclepiad, gentian, and loganiaceae families. Antirhea affinis is a species of flowering plant in the genus Antirhea, which contains several species of flowering plants found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The genus Antirhea is part of the tribe Spermacoceae, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The classification of Antirhea affinis highlights the complex and diverse relationships within the Rubiaceae family.
